subject: ENGLISH
title: Blend Builders
objective: Read taught consonant blends while keeping their component sounds perceptible.
introduction: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Blend Builders”.
A key word used in the lesson “Blend Builders”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Which word begins with the st blend?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. ship and top do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

options.text: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.

items.explanation: The correct response is “In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.” because it follows the lesson explanation: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.
The correct response is “flag begins with fl” because it follows the lesson explanation: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “ship begins with a blend” because it follows the lesson explanation: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.

items.prompt: Which word begins with the st blend?
items.explanation: The correct response is “stop” because it follows the lesson explanation: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “nest ends with st” because it follows the lesson explanation: In a consonant blend, neighboring consonant sounds can still be heard.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Blend Builders; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Blend Builders.
